foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Surgical nutrition addresses the catabolic phase of the metabolic response to injury; understanding ebb and flow phases of homeostasis guides when and how to initiate nutritional support

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Enteral and parenteral nutrition require concurrent fluid and electrolyte management; refeeding syndrome causes dangerous hypophosphatemia and hypokalemia

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Protein-calorie malnutrition impairs collagen synthesis and immune function, directly delaying wound healing and increasing dehiscence risk

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Malnutrition is the strongest modifiable risk factor for surgical site infections; albumin <3 g/dL significantly increases SSI rates

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: ERAS protocols mandate preoperative carbohydrate loading and early postoperative enteral feeding as core perioperative nutritional interventions
